53 And this I make an example unto you, for your consolation concerning all those who have been commanded to do a work and have been hindered by the hands of their enemies, and by oppression, saith the Lord your God.

“From early youth, Latter-day Saints have or should have been taught to regard work as honorable and to dignify it by performing an honest day's work for a fair day's pay. The poet Carlyle expressed this sentiment when he penned the lines: ‘All work, even cotton-spinning is noble; work alone is noble.’ The Apostle Paul clearly understood and emphasized the principle of work. In his epistle to the Thessalonians, he reminded them: ‘ ‘. . . this we commanded you, that if any would not work,…
10 For even when we were with. yon, this we commanded. you, that if. any would: not work, neither. should. he eat.

“Paul counseled the saints in Corinth ‘not to company with fornicators.’ (1 Corinthians 5:9.) He broadened the counsel to include not only fornicators but also ‘the covetous, or extortioners, or with idolators.’ “As President Harold B. Lee said, in order to help someone else ‘we must stand on higher ground’ than he is standing on. We must be careful not to abandon that ground—for the sake of the sinner as well as for our own welfare. There is a difference between assisting the wounded Samaritan…

16. Now the.. Lord of peace himself give you peace always by all means. The Lord be with you all.
Let us now most earnestly inquire, What preparations are necessary for man in order to meet the Lord in peace ?
באשר ה' אתו — means BECAUSE THE LORD WAS WITH HIM (i.e. באשר means because).
The expression אדני simply means "my lord," and does not represent a name of G'd.

-17- The. salutation. of Paul with mine own hand, which: is the token in-every epistle;: so.I write.:

It appears that Paul did not actually do the writing with his own hand, but dictated to a scribe, although he would sign the epistle himself. Hence we find in Rom. 16:22, "I Tertius, who wrote this epistle, salute you"; and in 1 Cor. 16:21, "The salutation of me Paul with mine own hand"; and in 2 Thes. 3:17, "The salutation of Paul with mine own hand, which is the token in every epistle: so I write" (see also Col. 4:18; Philem. 1:19; Gal. 6:11). (Robert J. Matthews, Behold the Messiah, p.325)
-17- The. salutation. of Paul with mine own hand, which: is the token in-every epistle;: so.I write.:

It appears that Paul did not actually do the writing with his own hand, but dictated to a scribe, although he would sign the epistle himself. Hence we find in Rom. 16:22, "I Tertius, who wrote this epistle, salute you"; and in 1 Cor. 16:21, "The salutation of me Paul with mine own hand"; and in 2 Thes. 3:17, "The salutation of Paul with mine own hand, which is the token in every epistle: so I write" (see also Col. 4:18; Philem. 1:19; Gal. 6:11). -17- The. salutation. of Paul with mine own hand, which: is the token in-every epistle;: so.I write.:

It appears that Paul did not actually do the writing with his own hand, but dictated to a scribe, although he would sign the epistle himself. Hence we find in Rom. 16:22, "I Tertius, who wrote this epistle, salute you"; and in 1 Cor. 16:21, "The salutation of me Paul with mine own hand"; and in 2 Thes. 3:17, "The salutation of Paul with mine own hand, which is the token in every epistle: so I write" (see also Col. 4:18; Philem. 1:19; Gal. 6:11). (Robert J. Matthews, Behold the Messiah, p.325)
worship [Gr.. proskuneo] a probable derivative of 'to kiss the hand in token of reverence; hence among
worshipped [proskuneo to kiss the hand to (towards) one, in token of reverence]

(Robert J. Matthews, Behold the Messiah, p.325)

 all until His coming in His kingdom. Amen.
